reasoning: The informal statement asserts: for any integers a, b, c, there exists a positive integer n such that √(n³+an²+bn+c) is not an integer. The Lean version uses Mathlib's Real.sqrt (which returns 0 for negative inputs) and asserts ∃ n > 0 such that no integer m satisfies Real.sqrt(P(n)) = ↑m. Under Mathlib's sqrt convention, ¬∃ m : ℤ, Real.sqrt(P(n)) = m is equivalent to "P(n) > 0 and P(n) is not a perfect square", which matches the standard mathematical reading of "√P(n) is not an integer" in the Putnam context. The quantifier structure (∀ a b c, ∃ n > 0, ...) matches exactly, n > 0 with n : ℤ correctly encodes "positive integer", and the polynomial expression n³+an²+bn+c is correctly coerced. The two statements are logically equivalent at the ∀∃ level.
